name: Nicotinamide Riboside (NR)
historicalUse: Nicotinamide riboside is a form of vitamin B3 (niacin) found in trace amounts in milk and yeast. As an isolated compound it has no classical herbal history, but niacin-rich foods — meat, fish, fermented foods — were recognized across medical traditions for their role in vitality and energy. The molecular understanding of NAD+ as the cellular currency of energy metabolism is a 20th-century discovery that illuminates why these classical dietary recommendations supported energy and longevity.
classicalSources: Empirical parallels in classical dietary recommendations for vitality
modernEvidence: NR is the most bioavailable oral precursor to NAD+, a coenzyme that declines by 50%+ with age. NAD+ is essential for all mitochondrial energy production, DNA repair, and sirtuin activation. Tru Niagen® (NR chloride) is the only NR form with GRAS status and the most extensively clinically studied NR product. Human trials demonstrate consistent 40–60% increases in blood NAD+ levels. Downstream effects include sirtuin activation, improved mitochondrial function, and DNA repair enhancement.
mechanisms: NAD+ precursor — NR → NMN → NAD+ via Preiss-Handler and salvage pathways
Sirtuin activation — NAD+-dependent SIRT1/3/6 regulate gene expression and metabolic health
PARP activity support — DNA repair enzymes require NAD+ as substrate
Mitochondrial biogenesis — NAD+/SIRT1/PGC-1α axis stimulates new mitochondrial synthesis
CD38 regulation — reducing CD38 activity preserves cellular NAD+ pools
benefits: Supports healthy NAD+ levels associated with aging
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Tru Niagen® GRAS status. Well tolerated with minimal adverse effects in clinical trials. Unlike niacin (nicotinic acid), NR does not cause flushing.
contraindications: Caution in patients with active cancer — NAD+ elevation may theoretically support cancer cell metabolism; consult oncologist
drugInteractions: Chemotherapy — consult oncologist; theoretical concern regarding NAD+ support of cancer cell DNA repair
No significant drug interactions identified in clinical studies to date
physicianNotes: Tru Niagen® is the preferred form — GRAS status, most clinical data, patent-protected stability. Monitor in oncology patients — theoretical concern about NAD+ supporting cancer cell metabolic activity warrants oncologist consultation before use. No flushing effect unlike standard niacin. Highly appropriate in anti-aging and mitochondrial medicine protocols.
patientGuidance: Take in the morning — NR supports energy metabolism and may be stimulating for some individuals. Unlike regular niacin (vitamin B3), NR does not cause skin flushing. If you are undergoing cancer treatment, discuss with your oncologist before use.
